subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Dark Energy: The Expanding Mystery
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Dark Energy: The Expanding Mystery.

hook.text: About 68% of the universe is dark energy—a mysterious force pushing the cosmos apart faster and faster.

buildup.text: It was discovered in 1998 when distant supernovae showed the universe's expansion is accelerating, not slowing down.

discovery.text: Nobody expected acceleration. The discovery won the Nobel Prize and remains one of the biggest surprises in modern physics.

twist.text: Dark energy might be a property of empty space—Einstein's cosmological constant, the idea he once called his greatest blunder.

climax.text: If dark energy strengthens, the universe could end in a Big Rip—tearing apart galaxies, stars, atoms, and spacetime itself.

punchline.text: Two-thirds of the universe is an unknown force. It decides the fate of everything.

quiz.question: What would happen if dark energy continues to strengthen over time?
options: It could cause a Big Rip, tearing apart galaxies, atoms, and spacetime itself
It would cause the universe to collapse back into a singularity
It would gradually convert all matter into pure energy
